cross_category_overview: Across all categories, regulators are visibly intensifying enforcement and broadening their remit — with AML/CTF, consumer protection, governance and director-duty actions (AUSTRAC, ASIC, ACMA, TEQSA, AMSA) running in parallel with a wave of structural reform (ATEC, EPBC streamlining, NDIS, super switching, CIRMP, Tranche 2) that is materially lifting compliance build-out costs and creating concentrated advisory demand for assurance, remediation and program design. Cutting across sectors, AI governance, operational/cyber resilience, and cross-agency fraud and integrity scrutiny are emerging as the dominant horizontal themes, pointing KPMG advisory toward integrated regulatory readiness, board-level governance uplift and AI-risk frameworks as the highest-growth engagement areas.

hub_wide.brief: Three regulator-investment themes dominate this fortnight: enforcement-led capability uplift (ASIC's Money3/ABL/Berndale/Canva actions, AUSTRAC's Tabcorp probe, ACMA's Entain undertaking, TEQSA's ANU intervention), tech and AI assurance build-out (CASA's AI transparency statement and myCASA migration, Melbourne Airport's AI emergency-ops, APRA/RBA frontier-AI cyber warnings, ATEC stand-up under Turvey), and infrastructure/network reset spend (AER's Victorian 2026-31 distribution determinations, NSW MMC building approvals, WA's $2bn housing package, Brennan/Macquarie sovereign cloud builds). Clients in the news this fortnight include Tabcorp, Entain/Ladbrokes/Neds, CitiPower/Powercor/United Energy/AusNet/Jemena, ANU, Money3, Canva, Melbourne Airport, Austal and Macquarie — each a live BD hook for existing account teams. Pent-up demand is clearest around AML/CTF and AFSL remediation, network revenue-reset PMOs, NDIS and aged-care provider readiness, DISP/Tranche 2 supply-chain assurance, and AI/regulator-tech assurance for transport and Commonwealth regulators. KPMG should concentrate BD effort this fortnight on three plays: a wagering/gaming AML+conduct remediation offer leveraging the Tabcorp-AUSTRAC and ACMA-Entain matters; a Victorian network revenue-reset PMO timed to the post-determination implementation window across the five DNSPs; and an integrated NDIS + Aged Care provider readiness diagnostic ahead of Butler's accountability push and the Vic Retirement Village law changes. Secondary pursuits — ATEC operating-model design, DISP/Tranche 2 SME assurance, and CIRMP uplift for airports/ports — should be staffed against named partners now to be in front of buyers before the next fortnightly sync.

client_news_weekly.brief: VIC: The Treasurer's Budget messaging this week led with community safety commitments through the Department of Treasury and Finance, signalling sustained appropriations across justice, emergency services and frontline policing. BD action: track agency-level allocations as they cascade in coming weeks for program assurance, workforce planning and ICT modernisation pull-through — particularly for Victoria Police, DJCS and Court Services Victoria. Federal: Enforcement and integrity dominated the cycle. ASIC issued multiple market-integrity actions, including a $792k infringement notice against Canva Group for late financial reporting, an AFS licence cancellation (ABL Funds Management), and a former Berndale director pleading guilty to dishonest conduct — reinforcing demand for licensee remediation, breach reporting and financial-reporting controls reviews. AUSTRAC is reportedly investigating Tabcorp, with a new enterprise agreement also published — flagging AML/CTF program uplift and workforce-capability hooks. AFP and ABF ran a heavy operational tempo: counter-terrorism online funding, illicit tobacco, JOCTF drug seizures, maritime interdictions and two new patrol boats — supporting capability assurance, data analytics and procurement advisory. Ahpra opened applications for an Expert Panel on Drug and Alcohol Screening and published commentary on specialist workforce sustainability; ACQSC reinforced rights-based aged care messaging. Cross-cluster themes: regulator enforcement intensity, AML/CTF and financial-crime uplift, frontline workforce sustainability, border and maritime capability, and rights-based service delivery in health and aged care. Partners should prime conversations on assurance over Budget-funded programs, AML remediation readiness, and panel/expert-advisory positioning ahead of FY27 procurement cycles.

TEQSA is simultaneously sharpening its enforcement posture — accepting a voluntary undertaking from ANU and taking effective control of the Chancellor selection process under Emeritus Professor Coaldrake, while publishing a refreshed Regulatory Risk Framework and a year-one review of centralised Enquiries Management. On the policy side, Treasury and DEWR are being pushed to accelerate financial-adviser education standard reforms and reduce sector regulatory burden, opening pull-through for capability reviews, governance assurance, and risk-framework uplift across universities and professional-standards bodies.
